C93_P1_ambient_forest.evidence: MerlinLairScene.ts:752 — startAmbient('forest') called immediately after loadLairGLBs() block (line 749). MerlinLairScene.ts:930 — stopAmbient() called as first statement in dispose(). SFXManager.ts handles suspended AudioContext via pendingAmbientType (line 328).

C93_P2_crystal_ball_glb.evidence: LairGLBAssets.ts:185-197 — crystal_ball.glb loader present. Position set(5, -1.0, -4) (line 193), scale 0.8 (line 194). On success: crystalSphere.visible = false (line 196). isDisposed guard at line 186. MerlinLairScene.ts:743 passes crystalSphere to loadLairGLBs. Comment at LairGLBAssets.ts:21 confirms hitTarget+light stay active.

evidence: MinigameBase.ts:219-221 — reads localStorage['merlin_mg_plays'], computes difficultyTier 0-3 in play().
MinigameBase.ts:247-249 — increments merlin_mg_plays counter in finish().

flat_shading_coherence.rationale: All MeshStandardMaterial instances in MerlinLairScene.ts (lines 38,39,57,63,69,98,130,150,183,238,310) carry flatShading:true. GLB materials cloned in LairGLBAssets.ts (lines 37,70,122,189) preserve the flat style set in Blender. Consistent low-poly aesthetic throughout procedural and GLB layers.
flat_shading_coherence.evidence: MerlinLairScene.ts:38 stoneMat flatShading:true; LairGLBAssets.ts:38 roughness=0.9 post-clone.

z_fighting_residual.rationale: sol_pierre.glb positioned at y=-4.98 (line 105 LairGLBAssets.ts), 0.02 above the procedural floor (y=-5), explicit z-bias avoidance. mur_pierre.glb uses polygonOffset factor=-2 units=-4 (lines 126-127) covering shallow camera angles (C90-P2 upgrade from -1/-2). No mortar line z-fight reported. Minor residual risk on back-wall mortar lines at -9.74 vs back wall at -9.76 (Δ=0.02) — within tolerance for most GPUs but could flicker on 16-bit depth Mali.
z_fighting_residual.evidence: LairGLBAssets.ts:105 y=-4.98; lines 125-127 polygonOffsetFactor=-2 polygonOffsetUnits=-4; MerlinLairScene.ts:87 mortar z=-9.74 vs back wall z=-10 (Δ=0.26 — safe).

material_quality.rationale: Cauldron material roughness=0.9, metalness=0.0 (LairGLBAssets.ts:38-39), preventing HDR specular spikes on low-end hardware. Crystal material: roughness=0.25, metalness=0.1, transparent opacity=0.82 (MerlinLairScene.ts:150-159) — readable glassy effect. Book material pool of 6 (line 185-186) uses 6 thematically distinct colors. Emissive hierarchy is consistent (baseline 0.0 on GLB bodies, C90-P1). One gap: bibliothèque.glb and table_druidique.glb receive no post-load material mutation — cannot verify roughness is compatible with ambient lighting without the actual GLB.

lighting_dramatism.rationale: 5-source lighting: key light (implicit hemisphere ambient), cauldron green glow PointLight(0x22cc44, 1.2, 3.5) (MerlinLairScene.ts:534), door warm sliver PointLight(0xffdd66, 3.0, 5) (line 271), crystal purple PointLight(0x9060cc, 2.5, 5, quadratic) (line 172), shared candle PointLight(0xff9933, 1.8, 9) with 3-frequency flicker (lines 327,409-412). Additive blending on particles (steam+candle). Multi-frequency candle flicker (7.3Hz + 13.1Hz + 3.7Hz sin) at line 409-412 creates organic animation. Excellent dramatic variety for the scene.

glb_coverage.rationale: 7 GLB overlays in LairGLBAssets.ts: cauldron_merlin, bougie (x3 instances), table_druidique, bibliotheque, sol_pierre, mur_pierre (InstancedMesh 3x), crystal_ball. All have isDisposed() guards. All have .catch() procedural fallback. All hide their procedural counterpart on success. C93-P2 adds crystal_ball completing the main interactive zones. Only gap: no ceiling or door GLB — procedural boxes remain.

geometric_proportions.rationale: Room is 24×20×16u (W×D×H) — lair-scale appropriate. Crystal pedestal CylinderGeometry(0.4, 0.55, 0.9, 8) + sphere SphereGeometry(0.7, 8, 6) are well-proportioned against the room (line 162-168). Bookshelf 3.5×5.5u frame at x=8 (line 190-191). Candle positions match GLB CANDLE_POSITIONS exactly (confirmed line 301-305 comment). Wall InstancedMesh row height ROW_H=16/3≈5.33u covers y=-5 to y=11 correctly (line 132-133). Minor: door width 3u at x=-10.1 is narrow relative to a 16u wall.

celtic_style.rationale: Low-poly aesthetic, earthy palette (0x3a3228 stone, 0x4a3520 wood, 0x201c18 floor), crystal ball, cauldron with green steam — all thematically correct. Candle flicker, floating dust motes (80 particles, MerlinLairScene.ts:446), scroll/map on table (mapMat emissive amber). Window + LairWindow forest view adds druidic depth. Celtic knotwork or Ogham carvings not visible in procedural geometry — depends on GLB content which cannot be verified from code.

bundle_gzip.rationale: Cannot measure bundle size from source alone. Code hygiene is good: shared geometries (shelfGeo reused line 195), material pool of 6 (line 185), InstancedMesh for walls (48 clones → 3 draw calls, line 137-179). Dust uses Float32Array typed arrays. No large string literals or embedded base64 detected. Risk: 7 async GLB fetches — gzip efficiency depends on GLB file sizes, not visible in code. Downgraded from potential 9 due to unverifiable GLB payload.

fps_budget.rationale: InstancedMesh reduces wall draw calls from 48 to 3 (LairGLBAssets.ts:111-179). Shared candle PointLight saves 2 light slots (MerlinLairScene.ts:325). Crystal PointLight range=5 (line 172) prevents bleeding (was 8, comment notes fix). Candle particle N=20 per candle × 3 = 60 particles total. Dust N=80. Steam N=30. Total ~170 Points particles — well within budget. Particle geo uses Float32Array with in-place mutation + needsUpdate (efficient). Risk: 7 simultaneous async GLB loads could spike main-thread on slow devices during initial load.

memory_leaks.rationale: dispose() at MerlinLairScene.ts:928 calls stopAmbient() (line 930), then traverses scene and calls geometry.dispose() + material.dispose() for all Mesh children (lines 940-944), then renderer.dispose() (line 948). Template materials (candleBaseMat, wickMat) disposed immediately after use at lines 398-399. C81-03 isDisposed flag prevents post-dispose scene.add() from orphaned GLB callbacks. C81-07 comment confirms this pattern. One minor risk: penaltyToasts array in mg_herboristerie is filtered but not nulled in cleanup() — however cleanup() calls super.cleanup() → container.innerHTML='' which removes canvas, and the animFrame is cancelled. Acceptable.

zone_feedback_aria.rationale: Four interactive zones (map/crystal/bookshelf/door/cauldron) have hover emissive boost and a 35ms shimmer tone (SFXManager playHover, line 107). No aria-label found on the 3D canvas element itself in MerlinLairScene.ts (canvas element accessed via renderer.domElement, not inspectable in this code). No role='application' or keyboard navigation on the THREE.js renderer canvas. The minigame canvases have aria properly (mg_sang_froid.ts:86-87, mg_herboristerie.ts:130-140) but the lair 3D viewport itself lacks it. This is the main accessibility gap.
zone_feedback_aria.evidence: SFXManager.ts:107; mg_sang_froid.ts:86-87; MerlinLairScene.ts — no aria-label on renderer canvas found.

game_feel_flow.rationale: Hover: emissive ramp + 35ms tone (non-intrusive). Click: zone callback + transition. Forest ambient loops immediately on entry (line 752), stops on dispose (line 930). Candle flicker at 3 frequencies creates living environment. Crystal pulse via emissiveIntensity 0.6 baseline. Cauldron steam rises with upward velocity + scatter. Good tactile chain: enter → ambient starts → see flickering candles → hover zone → emissive glow + shimmer → click → transition. Gap: no loading indicator while GLBs resolve — procedural↔GLB pop-in can break immersion.

celtic_immersion.rationale: Ambient forest audio ('forest' type at SFXManager.ts:384 — 65Hz drone + lowpass wind + bird chirps). Organic candle flicker, green cauldron glow, purple crystal, warm door light, dust motes. Scroll/map on druidic table. LairWindow adds forest view with day/night/season cycle (createLairWindow imported). High thematic coherence. Gap: no Ogham symbol geometry visible in procedural code — relies on GLB assets which may or may not contain carvings.

canvas_visual_quality.rationale: mg_sang_froid: radial gradients for zone glow + cursor glow, pulsing zone fill, concentric indicator rings, background grid, HUD overlay (lines 236-312). mg_herboristerie: linear bg gradient, breathing cell animation (sin at line 378), flash overlays, shake translate, floating penalty toasts (line 414-425). Both use clamp() for font-size responsive text. Solid production quality for canvas-based games.

animation_smoothness.rationale: Both games use getDeltaTime() (MinigameBase.ts:206-211) — real delta capped at 100ms, returns 1/60 on first frame. No fixed dt=1/60 hardcoding. requestAnimationFrame loop in both render() methods. mg_sang_froid drift uses dt multiplication (line 172). mg_herboristerie shake decays via *= 0.85 per frame (line 306). Flash alpha decays at dt*2 (line 311). All physics are frame-rate independent.

feedback_visual.rationale: mg_sang_froid: zone color flips green↔orange on inside/outside, zone pulse, cursor color feedback, line-to-center guide, HUD % live. mg_herboristerie: green/red flash overlay per cell, correct cells fade to 0.4 opacity, wrong cells show ✗, shake on wrong pick, floating −8 pts toast (C92-P2), score counter top-right. Outcome overlay (MinigameBase): 4-level badge + score + flavor text + WebAudio tone. Canvas dim to 0.25 opacity during overlay (line 258-260). Excellent feedback density.

mobile_responsive.rationale: Canvases have max-width:100% (mg_sang_froid.ts:91, mg_herboristerie.ts:134). touch-action:none on both canvases. pointerdown + pointermove events (unified pointer API). Timer bar width:min(380px,100%). Font-size:clamp() in outcome overlay (MinigameBase.ts:133,140). Cursor tracking uses rect.width scaling for DPR-aware coordinates (mg_sang_froid.ts:142-143). Gap: canvas is 380×380px logical — on 320px wide phones the canvasW=380 forces scroll unless CSS scales it, but max-width:100% should constrain it. Cell size 64px in herboristerie may be tight on small screens for touch targets (grid occupies 5×64=320px + 40px = 360px total — borderline).

timer_leak_free.rationale: mg_sang_froid: timerInterval cleared in both endGame() (line 149) and cleanup() (line 318). animFrame cancelled in both endGame() (line 150) and cleanup() (line 319). Ended flag guards double-endGame() (line 147). mg_herboristerie: same pattern (lines 281-284, 432-435). MinigameBase.finish() has idempotent guard 'finished' (line 242). getDeltaTime lastRenderMs reset to 0 in play() (line 216) preventing stale dt on replay.

audio_feedback.rationale: MinigameBase.ts: 4-level WebAudio tones — desastre sawtooth [110,100,90Hz], echec sine [220,196Hz], reussite [330,392,440Hz], maitrise [440,554,659,880Hz] (lines 81-86). ctx.resume() before playback (line 92, BUG-03 fix). Each play creates a fresh AudioContext (no shared ctx), which avoids state pollution but wastes a context object per outcome — minor. SFXManager provides hover shimmer (660Hz, 35ms, gain 0.06) at lair level. No in-game feedback sounds during gameplay (no tick per second, no zone-shrink warning sound). Gap vs 10: no mid-game audio.

hitbox_fairness.rationale: mg_sang_froid: hitbox is the exact circle (Math.sqrt(dx²+dy²) <= currentRadius, line 196-197) — pixel-perfect. Cursor tracked with DPR-aware scaling (lines 142-143). Zone bounce keeps center inside margins so zone never clips canvas edge (lines 176-184). mg_herboristerie: getCellAt() uses floor division with offsetX=20 offsetY=60 — exact grid mapping, no dead zones (lines 233-239). Touch coordinate scaling identical to display coordinates (lines 244-246). Fair.

difficulty_balance.rationale: C94 scaling verified: mg_sang_froid tier 0→3: endRadius 28→16px (28,24,20,16), driftSpeed 18→30px/s. At tier 3: 16px final radius on a 380px canvas — very small (~4% diameter) — challenging but achievable given 120px start. mg_herboristerie tier 0→3: totalTime 15→9s. With 6 targets out of 20 cells, 9s gives ~1.5s per target — tight but fair. Tier boundaries: 0-2 plays=tier0, 3-6=tier1, 7-11=tier2, 12+=tier3 — ramp is gradual (3+ sessions before tier1). One concern: tier starts computing at play() before setup() runs, so difficultyTier feeds correctly into setup() scaling.

instructions_clarity.rationale: mg_sang_froid title: 'SANG-FROID -- Reste dans la zone' + instruction 'Garde ton curseur dans le cercle. Il retrecit...' (lines 59-66). mg_herboristerie title: 'HERBORISTERIE -- Trouve les bonnes plantes' with target indicator showing emoji + name in canvas (line 339-351). Instructions are direct. Gap: no explanation of scoring formula, penalty cost, or tier system visible to player. 'Il retrecit...' truncated with ellipsis — missing info that zone also drifts. Accessible aria-label on canvases is set (mg_sang_froid:86-87, mg_herboristerie:140).

aria_accessibility.rationale: Both canvases have role='application', tabIndex=0 for keyboard focus, aria-label (lines 86-87 sf, 130-140 herb). Timer progressbar has role='progressbar', aria-valuemin/max/now updated live (mg_sang_froid:71-77). mg_herboristerie timer same (lines 114-120). Outcome overlay: role='status' aria-live='assertive' (MinigameBase.ts:118-119). Gaps: no keyboard control for cursor in mg_sang_froid (only pointer input), no keyboard cell selection in mg_herboristerie. Screen reader cannot play either game without pointer device.

variety_design.rationale: mg_sang_froid is a twitch/precision skill test (keep cursor in shrinking+drifting zone). mg_herboristerie is a visual search/identification task (scan grid, click correct emoji pattern). Mechanically distinct. 3 plant sets in herboristerie provide visual variety across plays (PLANT_SETS array lines 30-68). VS-15 text-presentation fix prevents color-emoji rendering anomaly on Android (C92-P1). mg_sang_froid has randomized drift direction changes (1.5-3.5s interval). Both loop-able and replayable. Gap: no minigame that tests rhythm, audio, or physical drawing — broader variety still possible.

lore_coherence.rationale: mg_sang_froid flavor: 'Les esprits de la forêt se détournent de toi' / 'Merlin hoche la tête — l'équilibre est maintenu' / 'Ogham vivant — la forêt chante à ton passage' — all thematically celtic/druidic (MinigameBase.ts:48-70). Plant names in herboristerie: Trèfle, Gui sacré, Verveine, Alchimille, Morelle noire, Aconit, Belladone, Ciguë, Digitale — authentic celtic herbalism vocabulary. Emoji set uses text symbols (☘ ☠ ⚙) forced to text-presentation, consistent with low-color aesthetic. Thematically coherent.

lair_hub.note: Sum=108/13=8.31 — driven down by zone_feedback_aria(7) and bundle_gzip(7)

minigames.note: Sum=99/12=8.25 — driven down by instructions_clarity(7) and aria_accessibility(7)

top_3_remaining_issues.title: 3D lair canvas has no ARIA role or label — keyboard users cannot identify interactive zones
top_3_remaining_issues.detail: The THREE.js renderer canvas (renderer.domElement) is added to the container in MerlinLairScene.ts without role='application', aria-label, or tabIndex. The 4+1 interactive zones (map/crystal/bookshelf/door/cauldron) provide only pointer-based hover feedback (emissive glow + hover tone). Screen readers and keyboard-only users have no interface for the hub.
top_3_remaining_issues.file_line: MerlinLairScene.ts — renderer.domElement insertion (search around line 870-920, no aria attributes present)
top_3_remaining_issues.recommendation: Add canvas.setAttribute('role','application'), canvas.setAttribute('aria-label','Antre de Merlin — 5 zones interactives'), canvas.tabIndex=0, and a keyboard handler mapping arrow keys + Enter to zone cycling.

top_3_remaining_issues.title: GLB assets have no load-progress indicator — procedural-to-GLB pop-in breaks immersion
top_3_remaining_issues.detail: loadLairGLBs() fires 7 async fetch+parse operations simultaneously (LairGLBAssets.ts:32,64,84,93,103,114,185). Each resolves independently and immediately hides its procedural counterpart. On slow connections (mobile 3G) the pop-in of table_druidique, bibliotheque, or mur_pierre will be visually jarring. There is no Promise.all progress callback, no loading spinner, and no fade-in transition on GLB scene insertion.
top_3_remaining_issues.file_line: LairGLBAssets.ts:32-197 — all .then() callbacks do scene.add() + visibility toggle with no transition
top_3_remaining_issues.recommendation: Wrap each gltf.scene in an opacity ramp (THREE.MeshStandardMaterial opacity 0→1 over 400ms) or add a global loaded/total progress callback to loadLairGLBs().

top_3_remaining_issues.title: mg_sang_froid: drift direction change is not communicated to the player — sudden pivot is perceptually unfair
top_3_remaining_issues.detail: At mg_sang_froid.ts:188-191, drift direction resets randomly every 1.5–3.5s without any visual telegraph. At tier 3 (driftSpeed=30px/s), a sudden 180-degree pivot gives the player ~0.5s to react before the zone exits their cursor. No particle trail or predictive arrow indicates the upcoming pivot. The bounce logic at lines 176-184 is solid, but the midfield random pivot is abrupt.
top_3_remaining_issues.file_line: mg_sang_froid.ts:186-191 — nextDriftChange pivot, no visual cue
top_3_remaining_issues.recommendation: Add a brief brightening pulse to the zone border (1–2 frames of increased strokeStyle opacity) 0.3s before the drift pivot fires, or add a small velocity-direction arrow rendered inside the zone circle.

regression_check_vs_c93.memory_leaks: NO REGRESSION — C93 dispose() pattern preserved, C93-P1 stopAmbient() added
regression_check_vs_c93.glb_coverage: IMPROVEMENT — C93-P2 crystal_ball added (was missing in C93)
regression_check_vs_c93.difficulty_balance: IMPROVEMENT — C94 adds progressive difficulty ramp (was flat in C93)
regression_check_vs_c93.z_fighting: NO REGRESSION — C90-P2 polygonOffset factor=-2 maintained

verdict.reason: Both composites fall below the 8.5 threshold (Lair 8.31, Minigames 8.25). No regressions detected vs C93 — all three C93/C94 fixes verified. The two blocking factors are: (1) absent ARIA on the 3D lair canvas (zone_feedback_aria=7, aria_accessibility=7) and (2) instructions_clarity=7 across minigames. No CRITICAL issues (no softlocks, no memory leaks, no timer leaks). Escalation is NOT required.
priority_fixes: Add aria-label + role='application' + tabIndex to MerlinLairScene renderer canvas
Add keyboard zone cycling (arrow keys + Enter) for lair hub navigation
Improve mg_sang_froid instructions to mention zone drift + add visual pivot telegraph
Add GLB load fade-in transition to eliminate pop-in on slow connections
